records.ety: [L. deprecatus, p. p. of deprecari to avert by prayer, to deprecate; de- + precari to pray. See Pray.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deprecated (-kā`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Deprecating (-kā`tng).]

defs: To protest against; to advance reasons against.
quotes: His purpose was deprecated by all round him, and he was with difficulty induced to adandon it.

defs: To disapprove of strongly; to express a low opinion of.
